title: Centaurium breviflorum
intro: Centaurium breviflorum, also known as short-flowered centaury, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium breviflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dry extract, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Centaurium breviflorum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales and class Magnoliopsida. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is also known as Short-flowered Centaury. It is a member of the genus Centaurium, which is characterized by its distinctive flowers and growth habits. The genus Centaurium is further classified within the tribe Gentianeae.
